raw.population: [ADULT] UNKNOWN [CHILD] N: 48 Sex: m=21, f=27 Age: 7 ±3 (1.9 – 14) WT: 24.2 ±11.4 (9.1-57) (Cloyd et al. 1993)
raw.objective: “to determine optimal intravenous (i.v.) loading dose, and i.v. and oral VPA maintenance regimens”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, Induction status, formulation. Included: AGE, Induction status, formulation.
raw.observations: A 5 min, 15mg/kg dose result in total and free plasma [VPA]: CHILD= ~65, 7.5 mg/l ADULT= ~80 and 11 mg/l Induction status had little effect. Study shows a two-fold higher IV and oral dose may be required in induced patients. The simulation shows a total [VPA] increasing less than proportionally with dose, while unbound [VPA] increase linearly with dose, this may be due to nonlinear protein binding.
raw.dose: Steady state, IV and oral doses in patients
raw.structure: One-compartment model with first-order absorption and elimination; 2-binding site model for relation between total and bound/unbound concentrations
raw.equations: N1= theta1*exp(eta1) N2= theta2*exp(eta2) K1= theta3*exp(eta3) K2= theta4*exp(eta4) ---ADULT--- KA= theta5 V =theta6*WT CL= (theta7*WT)/1000 #Uninduced, converted from mL/h/kg to L/h CL= (theta8*WT)/1000 #Induced, converted from mL/h/kg to L/h ---CHILD--- KA=theta5 V= theta6*WT CL=(theta7*WT)/1000 #Uninduced, converted from mL/h/kg to L/h CL=(theta8*WT)/1000 #Induced, converted from mL/h/kg to L/h ALAG1=2 CB=(N1*K1*CU*ALB)/(1+(K1*CU))+((N2*K2*CU*ALB)/(1+(K2*CU)) CTOTAL=CU+CB Cipred=CTOTAL Cobs=Cipred #no error model reported CU: unbound VPA concentrations CB: bound VPA concentrations N1/2: number of binding sites K1/2: binding affinity constants ALB: albumin concentration
raw.parameter_values: [ADULT] theta1=1.54 theta2=0.194 theta3=11.9 theta4=164 theta5=0.1 theta6=1.31 theta7=65 theta8=110 omega1=0.0049 omega2=0.1629 omega3=0.0279 omega4=0.7392 [CHILD] theta1=1.54 theta2=0.194 theta3=11.9 theta4=164 theta5=0.1 theta6=1.81 theta7=130 theta8=220 omega1=0.0049 omega2=0.1629 omega3=0.0279 omega4=0.7392

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
